title: Harley Lyrics (selections)
summary: The checked-in HarLyr XML is a repository copy of the University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item, which represents thirty-one newly made transcriptions of Harley lyrics, including one lyric that alternates Middle English and Anglo-Norman. The transcriptions were prepared from N. R. Ker's 1965 EETS monochrome facsimile under Frances McSparran's direction and corrected by Meredith Jones; they are not keyed from G. L. Brook's 1948 edition. The University of Virginia Electronic Text Center later imposed numbering and printed pagination conforming to Brook, which explains the sequence 1-18 and 20-32 and pages 29-72. Brook's number 19, the twenty-line multilingual ‘Dum ludis floribus’ at f. 76r, is omitted in full even though its last two lines are English. The 1965 facsimile reproduces only Harley ff. 49-140 and 142 plus Royal 12 C XII ff. 68v and 76v, but it covers every selected HarLyr witness passage. No lawful complete public online copy of that facsimile, and no current public Harley 2253 image set, was found. The British Library's complete 264-canvas Royal 12 C XII IIIF object is cached separately at practical access resolution because it includes Ker's two ancillary Royal leaves; neither leaf transmits HarLyr-selected text. Exact OTA transcript files and a later complete METS scholarly edition are also cached as distinct resources; none is evidence that HarLyr derives its readings from Brook.
review_notes: The primary subject is an edited digital selection of thirty-one heterogeneous anonymous lyrics, not one authored medieval work, all lyrics in Harley 2253, the complete Brook edition, the complete Ker facsimile, or the whole physical codex.

The selected lyric witnesses occupy discontiguous passages on Harley ff. 59v, 62v-63v, 66r-67r, 70v-73r, 75r-76r, 79r-83r, 106r, 114v-115r, and 128r-128v. Those folio surfaces also contain adjacent material, so the locators are not blanket content claims.

The U-M XML contains 1,563 L elements. The exact OTA deposit contains 1,562: the U-M form adds the line ‘ywraht wes of þe beste’ as line 34 in Ker item 46 and moves ‘Ientil iolyf so þe Iay’ from the OTA's duplicate line 34 to line 41, matching the later METS text. The precise revision history is undocumented.

Eleven U+FFFD replacement characters in the repository XML correspond to eleven legacy high bytes in the exact OTA payload. They are preserved as a documented encoding defect rather than silently reconstructed.

The source history says manuscript-joined words were segmented with a colon, for example ‘itelle’ represented as ‘i:telle.’ The editorial-practice vocabulary has no word-segmentation category, so this convention is documented here rather than mislabeled as punctuation normalization.

The legacy XML CREATION value ‘c. 1310’ is unsourced and is not adopted as either a common composition date or the witness date. The current British Library catalog dates the relevant manuscript part to circa 1330-1340; individual lyric composition dates remain unresolved.

Physical-codex coverage, printed-facsimile coverage, selected-work coverage, and digital-file coverage are recorded separately. In particular, the Royal folios in EETS 255 have no HarLyr target-text overlap.

The complete Royal 12 C XII IIIF bundle retains all 264 exposed surfaces from front cover through spine at 1800-pixel access resolution. Its work-portion mapping points only to Ker's ancillary ff. 68v and 76v and explicitly records zero target-work overlap.

Rights statements are resource-specific. U-M's current No Copyright-US display, OTA's CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license, the older embedded personal-scholarly-use notice, and the pinned CME repository's software-worded Unlicense are not collapsed into one blanket permission.
Negative access findings are dated 2026-07-14 and do not prove that licensed, private, onsite, or subsequently restored copies do not exist.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for HarLyr
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by the repository publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 77,060 bytes at the pinned commit and has internal IDNO HarLyr.
The source submodule is evidence only and is not modified by this manifest.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item HarLyr
entities.description: A public structured text of thirty-one fresh manuscript-facsimile transcriptions, numbered and paginated to agree with Brook's 1948 edition but explicitly allowed to differ from Brook's readings.
notes: U-M exposes thirty-one table-of-contents entries numbered 1-18 and 20-32.
The structured file has 31 DIV1, 31 HEAD, 1,563 L, 44 PB, and 45 EPB elements; printed page milestones run continuously from 29 through 72.
Brook 28 / Ker 69 alternates Middle English and Anglo-Norman lines even though the legacy root language declaration records only Middle English.
The XML header says printed front matter, back matter, and editorial notes were omitted.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive deposit 1681, Harley Lyrics (selections)
entities.description: The public OTA deposit underlying the U-M item: a legacy SGML-like payload of the same thirty-one selected lyrics plus a separate header file.
notes: The exact 72,006-byte payload contains 31 div0 elements and 1,562 l elements.
Its eleven legacy non-UTF-8 high bytes align with the eleven replacement characters in the U-M-derived repository XML.

entities.label: Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253 (EETS Original Series 255)
entities.description: N. R. Ker's 1965 monochrome printed facsimile, the expressly named transcription source for the selected lyrics.

bibliographic.citation: N. R. Ker, ed., Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253, Early English Text Society, Original Series 255 (London and New York: Oxford University Press for EETS, 1965), xxiii, [194] pp.
bibliographic.title: Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 255
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: Reproduces Harley 2253 ff. 49r-140v and 142r-v, plus Royal 12 C XII ff. 68v and 76v; it does not reproduce all physical leaves of either codex.
All thirty-one HarLyr-selected poems fall within the reproduced Harley portion.

entities.label: G. L. Brook's The Harley Lyrics (1948)
entities.description: Brook's edition of the Middle English lyrics in Harley 2253. Its lyric numbering and printed pagination organize HarLyr, but the HarLyr header expressly says the transcriptions were newly made from the 1965 facsimile and may differ from Brook.

bibliographic.citation: G. L. Brook, ed., The Harley Lyrics: The Middle English Lyrics of MS. Harley 2253, Manchester University Publications, English Series 302 (Manchester: Manchester University Press, 1948).
bibliographic.title: The Harley Lyrics: The Middle English Lyrics of MS. Harley 2253

notes: HarLyr preserves Brook numbers 1-18 and 20-32. Brook number 19 is excluded, not accidentally renumbered.
No claim is made that Brook supplied HarLyr's textual readings.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2253
entities.description: A composite parchment codex. The relevant second part, ff. 49r-142v, was copied principally by the Harley scribe near Ludlow about 1330-1340 and contains Middle English, Anglo-Norman, and Latin material. HarLyr selects thirty-one English lyrics from discontiguous passages within that part.

date_statements.label: relevant manuscript part
date_statements.notes: This dates the relevant witness part, not every lyric's composition.

holding.notes: The child record for the relevant second part is https://searcharchives.bl.uk/catalog/041-002352751.

physical_description.extent: 1* + 142 folios plus flyleaves; relevant second part ff. 49r-142v
notes: The selected lyric witnesses occupy ff. 59v, 62v-63v, 66r-67r, 70v-73r, 75r-76r, 79r-83r, 106r, 114v-115r, and 128r-128v.
Brook number 19 / Ker item 55 begins at f. 76r but is not part of the HarLyr selection.

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 12 C XII
entities.description: A parchment manuscript copied mainly by the Harley scribe in or near Hereford about 1320-1340. Ker's 1965 facsimile includes only ff. 68v and 76v as ancillary same-scribe material; neither folio transmits a HarLyr-selected lyric.

entities.label: British Library IIIF facsimile of Royal MS 12 C XII
entities.description: The complete public 264-canvas IIIF image sequence for Royal MS 12 C XII, from front cover through spine. It is a supporting digital surrogate because Ker's 1965 printed facsimile reproduces Royal ff. 68v and 76v; neither leaf contains HarLyr-selected text.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and page-image services
notes: The ignored local research bundle contains all 264 exposed canvases at a practical 1800-pixel-wide access resolution; completeness and resolution are recorded separately.
This facsimile is not upstream of HarLyr and does not substitute for unavailable Harley 2253 images or Ker's 1965 printed facsimile.

entities.label: British Library digital facsimile of Harley MS 2253
entities.description: The official catalog reports full digital coverage for Harley 2253 but currently marks the images unavailable. This entity records that unavailable image layer without claiming a live public facsimile or inferring an undocumented IIIF endpoint.
entities.reproduction_format: Cataloged complete manuscript page-image coverage; current delivery unavailable

entities.label: The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript
entities.description: A later three-volume Middle English Texts Series edition and translation of the complete manuscript contents. It is useful collateral for boundary and damaged-character research but is not upstream of the 1992-1993 HarLyr encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Susanna Fein, ed., with David Raybin and Jan Ziolkowski, The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, 3 vols. (Kalamazoo, MI: Medieval Institute Publications, 2014-2015).
bibliographic.title: The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript

notes: The edition prints the manuscript's contents in manuscript order and therefore supplies wider codex context than the HarLyr selection.
The cached PDF is a modern scholarly edition and translation, not a photographic facsimile of Harley 2253.

agents.name: Frances McSparran
notes: Directed the University of Michigan student transcription project and is named as machine-readable-text editor.

agents.name: Meredith Jones
notes: Corrected the student transcriptions and is named as OTA editor.

agents.name: University of Virginia Electronic Text Center

scope.notes: Textual sequence, bibliographic metadata, replacement-byte alignment, and deposit history identify the versions. U-M has one additional L element in Ker item 46; the exact transformation mechanics are undocumented.

object.description: Facsimile images corresponding to the selected lyrics

scope.notes: The deposit header expressly calls these new transcriptions made from the EETS facsimile.

subject.description: Added lyric numbering, line references, and printed pagination

object.description: Brook lyric numbers 1-18 and 20-32 on pp. 29-72

scope.notes: This is a locator and organization relation only. The header credits the University of Virginia Electronic Text Center with the added numbering and pagination and says the new transcriptions may differ from Brook; it therefore does not support encoded_from.

subject.description: The Harley portion of the printed facsimile

object.description: Harley ff. 49r-140v and 142r-v; complete for all HarLyr-selected lyric witnesses but partial for the physical codex

subject.description: Two ancillary plates included in the printed facsimile

object.description: Royal ff. 68v and 76v only; no HarLyr-selected work content

subject.description: All 264 canvases in the complete public IIIF sequence

object.description: Complete physical codex as exposed from front cover through spine

scope.notes: Complete exposed-surface coverage does not imply preservation-master resolution. Ker's two ancillary leaves are mapped within this whole object, and neither contains target text.

subject.description: Cataloged full digital coverage, currently unavailable for delivery

scope.notes: The catalog supports existence and coverage, not current public access. No live image endpoint is asserted.

object.description: Complete manuscript contents in manuscript order

scope.notes: This 2015 edition is later collateral evidence and cannot be an upstream source of the 1992 OTA deposit.

notes: This is access to a physical printed copy, not a public digital facsimile.

access.access_method: Searched publisher/library catalogs, Open Library, Google Books, Internet Archive, HathiTrust discovery, and general web discovery; no lawful complete downloadable or viewable copy was located
notes: Catalog and snippet access is not a complete facsimile and is not cached as if it were one.

notes: No restricted file was downloaded or bypassed.

The current official catalog reports full digital coverage but states that digital images are currently unavailable; the legacy Digitised Manuscripts route no longer supplies a live complete object
Restricted reading-room access subject to British Library registration, advance request, and the catalog's letter-of-introduction conditions
Public IIIF Presentation 3 manifest with 264 canvases

local_copies.label: Royal 12 C XII IIIF manifest
notes: The local copy is the complete provider manifest, not an image bundle. Ker's ancillary f. 68v is canvas 145 and f. 76v is canvas 161; neither contains HarLyr target text.

local_copies.label: Complete Royal MS 12 C XII IIIF bundle (1800px access derivatives)
notes: All 264 canvases exposed by the provider manifest are retained at a practical non-upscaling 1800px-wide IIIF access resolution; this is not a preservation-master reproduction.
The exposed sequence includes front cover through spine.
ZIP contains the provider manifest, an exact-source inventory, and all 264 canvas images listed when retrieved.
Effective IIIF image request profile(s): "1800,", "full"; format: jpg.

work_portion.label: Ker 1965 ancillary Royal leaves (no HarLyr-selected work text)
locators: Royal MS 12 C XII, f. 68v; IIIF canvas 145
Royal MS 12 C XII, f. 76v; IIIF canvas 161
notes: These two leaves are supporting evidence for the scope of Ker’s printed facsimile; neither contains any of the thirty-one HarLyr-selected lyric witnesses.

access.access_method: Direct combined-edition PDF from the METS edition page

local_copies.label: Complete combined METS edition PDF
notes: Complete for the provider's combined three-volume edition, not a photographic facsimile and not an upstream HarLyr source.

rights.component: Underlying medieval wording of the thirty-one selected lyrics
rights.basis: The selected texts were fixed in a fourteenth-century manuscript and their underlying medieval wording is far outside the United States copyright term; this does not determine rights in modern transcriptions, editions, photographs, or metadata.

The current U-M item displays a DPLA No Copyright-United States statement. The older embedded XML notice limits use to personal scholarship, so this record treats the current assertion as provider evidence rather than an unconditional independent legal conclusion.
The OTA record makes the public files available under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported.

rights.component: Checked-in XML serialization and encoded modern editorial labor
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository has an Unlicense file worded for software, while the represented U-M/OTA materials carry different current and embedded notices. Without a source-specific clarification, the Unlicense is not treated as conclusively relicensing every transcription, markup, or supplied note in HarLyr.

rights.component: Brook's modern editorial matter, selection, notes, and typography
rights.basis: A 1948 United States publication could remain protected if copyright formalities and renewal were satisfied. No open license or conclusive renewal record was established, and the discovered complete scan is controlled borrowing.

rights.component: Ker introduction, photographic reproductions, selection, and printed layout
rights.basis: The 1965 publication may remain protected; no open license or conclusive public-domain determination was found. Underlying medieval text status does not determine rights in 1965 photographs or editorial matter.

rights.component: Any current or restored British Library digital images
rights.basis: The official record currently exposes no image object or image-specific rights statement. Rights for Royal 12 C XII cannot be transferred to a different manuscript object.

rights.component: Royal 12 C XII IIIF images, including ff. 68v and 76v
rights.jurisdiction: Countries other than the United Kingdom, as stated by the provider
rights.basis: The current IIIF manifest states ‘Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK.’ This is scoped to the Royal object and does not cover Harley 2253 or the 1965 facsimile.

rights.component: Royal 12 C XII IIIF images, including ff. 68v and 76v
rights.basis: The provider's manifest expressly excepts the United Kingdom from its public-domain statement; no broader license is inferred.

rights.component: Modern METS edition, translation, introduction, notes, and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: METS states that its digital editions are for individual use and that reprinting requires permission; the separate CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 offer for downloadable XML is not applied to the cached PDF.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Brook's numbered Middle English lyric sequence and the broader multilingual Harley miscellany
editorial_practices.representation: Thirty-one complete lyric transcriptions numbered 1-18 and 20-32
editorial_practices.description: The deposit selects thirty-one English lyrics. It omits Brook number 19, ‘Dum ludis floribus,’ in full rather than retaining its two English closing lines, but preserves Brook's subsequent numbering.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete OTA deposit and corresponding HarLyr body
notes: The gap is a documented catalog boundary, not a missing DIV1 caused by structural corruption.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Front matter, back matter, and printed editorial notes in Brook and Ker
editorial_practices.representation: Lyric texts with inherited numbering, line references, and page milestones
editorial_practices.description: The U-M header explicitly says front matter, back matter, and printed editorial notes were omitted. The body therefore does not represent either source volume as a complete digital edition.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/HarLyr.xml, pinned CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: IDG, sourceDesc, editorial declarations, and complete BODY
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the thirty-one-item body, U-M and OTA history, fresh facsimile transcription, University of Virginia addition of Brook-derived numbering and pagination, omitted print matter, bilingual Ker item 69, and repository-file identity.

evidence.citation: Structural and byte-level comparison of repository HarLyr.xml, exact OTA payload 1681, and cached METS article 46 context.
evidence.locator: BODY/DIV1, L, PB, EPB; OTA div0/l; replacement-byte positions
evidence.summary: Counts 31 DIV1, 31 HEAD, 1,563 L, 44 PB, and 45 EPB in U-M XML; verifies continuous local line numbering and Brook numbers 1-18, 20-32; identifies eleven U+FFFD characters aligned to OTA legacy bytes; and isolates the one-line U-M/OTA count difference in Ker item 46.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, ‘The Harley lyrics,’ HarLyr table of contents and item record.
evidence.locator: Item header, rights display, and thirty-one table-of-contents entries
evidence.summary: Confirms public availability, McSparran and Brook responsibilities as displayed, the 1965 and 1948 source dates, the thirty-one entries numbered 1-18 and 20-32, and U-M's current No Copyright-US assertion.

evidence.locator: Record metadata, files, availability, and license
evidence.summary: Confirms the public deposit, title, handle, exact downloadable files, and CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive deposit 1681, harlyr-1681.txt, exact downloaded payload.
evidence.locator: Complete file; 31 div0 and 1,562 l elements
evidence.summary: Exact legacy payload for all thirty-one selected lyrics; establishes selection order, the line-count difference from U-M, and the non-UTF-8 bytes corresponding to replacement characters.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive deposit 1681, header1681.xml, exact downloaded header.
evidence.locator: Creation, deposit, editorial, and source metadata
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1992-04-03 deposit, McSparran's machine-readable transcription role, Jones's correction role, and the EETS facsimile source.

evidence.citation: Catalog records for N. R. Ker, Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253, EETS OS 255 (1965), including British Library MS Facsimile 764, Folger record 83436, and Open Library OL16647572M.
evidence.summary: Confirms 1965 publication data, EETS series number 255, physical extent, the scope Harley ff. 49-140 and 142 plus Royal 12 C XII ff. 68v and 76v, and requestable printed-copy access. Discovery checks found catalog/snippet access but no lawful complete public digital copy.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive and Open Library record for G. L. Brook, The Harley Lyrics (Manchester University Press, 1948).
evidence.summary: Confirms Brook's 1948 bibliographic identity and controlled-borrow status; the protected derivatives were not downloaded.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253, parent record 040-002048084.
evidence.locator: Contents, physical description, access conditions, and digital-availability notice
evidence.summary: Confirms the composite codex, its two main parts, support and dimensions, trilingual contents, restricted physical access, cataloged full digital coverage, and the current statement that those digital images are unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253 second part, ff. 49r-142v, child record 041-002352751.
evidence.locator: Date, origin, contents list, and lyric folio assignments
evidence.summary: Dates the relevant part to circa 1330-1340 near Ludlow and supplies item-level folio locations for the selected lyrics and for the excluded Ker item 55 at f. 76r.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 12 C XII, record 040-002106725.
evidence.locator: Date, origin, scribal attribution, physical description, and digital object
evidence.summary: Confirms Royal 12 C XII's circa 1320-1340 date, Hereford association, principal Harley-scribe hand, 123-folio extent, and live IIIF digital object.

evidence.citation: British Library / Digirati, IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for Royal MS 12 C XII.
evidence.locator: 264 canvases; f. 68v canvas 145; f. 76v canvas 161; rights field
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete current Royal digital sequence, exact canvases for Ker's two ancillary plates, and the provider statement that the images are public domain in most countries other than the UK.

evidence.citation: Eric Stanley, ‘What Six Unalike Lyrics in British Library MS Harley 2253 Have Alike in Manuscript Layout,’ in Middle English Texts in Transition.
evidence.locator: Chapter summary identifying Brook 19 at f. 76r
evidence.summary: Identifies Brook number 19 as the macaronic ‘Dum ludis floribus’ on f. 76r, supporting the reason that a Middle-English-only selection can omit the whole item while retaining Brook's numbering.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, article 55, ‘Dum ludis floribus.’
evidence.summary: Shows that the excluded poem has eighteen Latin/Anglo-Norman lines followed by two English lines. HarLyr omits all twenty lines, establishing an item-level selection boundary rather than a language-filtered excerpt.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, three-volume digital edition and combined PDF.
evidence.locator: Edition landing page and complete 1,448-page PDF
evidence.summary: Confirms the later complete scholarly edition's scope, editors, manuscript-order organization, and downloadable combined PDF; used only as collateral evidence.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, ‘Copyright and Citation.’
evidence.locator: Digital-edition individual-use and reprint terms; XML license statement
evidence.summary: States that digital editions are for individual use and reprinting requires contact, while separately offering XML under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. The cached combined PDF is governed conservatively by the former terms.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright (revised April 2026).
evidence.summary: Official general legal reference supporting conservative treatment of the 1948 and 1965 modern publications; it does not itself establish their renewal histories.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: Complete Unlicense text, including software-oriented wording
evidence.summary: Provides the repository-level Unlicense evidence but does not expressly resolve how it applies to inherited transcriptions, metadata, or provider-originated editorial matter.

open_questions.question: Who made the structural and textual changes between the 1992 OTA payload and the 1993 U-M encoding, especially the added line in Ker item 46?
next_steps: Ask U-M and OTA for conversion logs or deposit correspondence.
Compare any preserved intermediate SGML/XML versions and timestamps.

open_questions.question: What exact characters should replace the eleven U+FFFD positions inherited from legacy OTA high bytes?
next_steps: Inspect the 1965 facsimile or an authorized reproduction at each affected line.
Consult OTA encoding documentation before mapping byte values.
Record any correction as a separate textual intervention rather than silently altering the source XML.

open_questions.question: Is a lawful complete public digital copy of Ker's 1965 EETS facsimile available, or can the selected plates be obtained under clear reuse terms?
next_steps: Ask EETS, the British Library, and holding libraries about licensed digital access or reproduction orders.
Recheck major digitization platforms and catalogs periodically.
notes: The complete later METS edition is not a substitute photographic source.

open_questions.question: Will the British Library restore public digital images of Harley 2253, and under what image-specific terms?
next_steps: Monitor the official parent and child records.
Ask the British Library about restoration and reproduction-order options for the exact selected folios.

open_questions.question: What composition dates, places, authorships, and individual genre or subject assignments can be established for each of the thirty-one heterogeneous lyrics?
next_steps: Research each Ker/BL catalog item individually against current scholarship and refrain from assigning one composition context to the selection as a whole.

open_questions.question: How should U-M's current No Copyright-US display, OTA's CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license, the embedded personal-scholarly-use notice, and the CME Unlicense be reconciled for redistribution of the repository XML?
next_steps: Request source-specific rights clarification from U-M, OTA, and the CME repository maintainer before commercial or unrestricted redistribution.
Keep Brook, Ker, BL image, and METS rights separate from the medieval text and digital transcription layers.
